pi_items.source: Verified by reading setup_task.sh source code: data is generated by an embedded Python script using purely arithmetic formulas (no random module). Confirmed via live testing: CSV has exactly 501 lines (1 header + 500 data rows), 25 unique students, 5 subjects, 4 quarters.

pi_items.source: Confirmed from task description and verifier.py/export_result.sh logic. The export script computes Q1 avg and Q4 avg per student (averaging across all 5 subjects) and checks (q1_avg - q4_avg > 10) OR (overall_attendance_avg < 75). Verified by independent computation on actual CSV data.

Independently computed from actual CSV captured from live environment. Confirmed by three independent computations: (1) live VM Python execution, (2) export_result.sh ground truth JSON, (3) local offline recomputation from captured CSV. All three agree exactly.

Independently computed Q4 averages: Math=79.04, Science=76.04, English=80.04, History=74.04, Art=78.04. History has the lowest Q4 average at 74.04. Confirmed by three independent computations.
Independently computed from actual CSV. Cross-verified against export_result.sh output which produced identical values.

Pearson r between per-student average StudyHoursWeekly and per-student average Score (all quarters, all subjects), computed using standard formula r = sum((x-mx)(y-my)) / sqrt(sum((x-mx)^2) * sum((y-my)^2)). n=25 students (all have at least one non-NA StudyHoursWeekly value). Confirmed by three independent computations: live VM (0.3809), export script (0.3809), local offline (0.3809).

Independently computed from captured CSV. Low-attendance students (Fatima Al-Rashid, Liam O'Brien) have values in the 65-75 range; stable students in 86-98 range.

privileged_info_summary: The CSV file at /home/ga/Documents/class_records.csv is semicolon-delimited with 500 data rows (25 students, 5 subjects: Math/Science/English/History/Art, 4 quarters: Q1-Q4) and 22 NA values in the StudyHoursWeekly column. There are exactly 5 at-risk students: Carlos Mendoza (Q1 avg 80.4, Q4 avg 65.4, drop 15.0 points), Fatima Al-Rashid (Q1 avg 79.4, Q4 avg 79.4, attendance 67.5% which is below 75%), Yuki Tanaka (Q1 avg 78.4, Q4 avg 66.4, drop 12.0 points), Priya Sharma (Q1 avg 76.4, Q4 avg 58.4, drop 18.0 points — steepest decline), and Liam O'Brien (Q1 avg 77.4, Q4 avg 65.4, drop 12.0 points AND attendance 71.5% — meets both criteria). The Q4 subject averages are: English 80.04, Math 79.04, Art 78.04, Science 76.04, History 74.04 — History has the lowest Q4 average. The Pearson correlation between StudyHoursWeekly and average Score is r = 0.3809 (moderate positive correlation, n=25). The agent must produce three files: class_analysis.py (Python script), class_dashboard.html (HTML with tables and red highlighting for at-risk students), and progress_report.odt (Sugar Write document titled 'Q4 Progress Report' with a table of at-risk students and a statement of the lowest Q4 subject and correlation value). The ODT must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/ (not just the Sugar Journal).
